summary_oneline: Harden a shipped tactical credential-leak fix into the brainstorm's strategic contract: allowlist REPL gate, a single Gemini final-response chokepoint, and a policy-driven OutputScrubber on the AbstractTool seam
synthesis_summary: A production credential leak (gemini-3 JiraSpecialist dumping os.environ via python_repl to Telegram + CloudWatch) has already received a TACTICAL fix, committed on dev in 0f76129b1 [F010]: python_repl gained an AST DENYLIST (BLOCKED_IMPORTS/NAMES/ATTRIBUTES + _check_ast_security) and output redaction [F002], and redact_text/redact_secrets from a new core module security/redaction.py [F005] were sprinkled across ~14 sites in the Gemini client [F003]. That fix closes the known vector but DIVERGES from the brainstorm's strategic design on all three workstreams: it is a denylist not an allowlist-first PythonCodeSanitizer (WS1) [F002]; redaction is scattered with no single _resolve_final_response chokepoint and default_api hunting/forced-synthesis-skip untouched (WS2) [F003]; and redaction.py is a flat-marker standalone module, not a policy-driven OutputScrubber hooked at the single AbstractTool.execute seam, and not built on the existing shell_tool security engine (WS3 + foundation) [F004, F005, F001]. FEAT-252 should consolidate the tactical fix into the brainstorm's design and decide, per workstream, how far to go.

localization.role: WS1 — shipped DENYLIST gate + in-process output redaction (to evolve toward allowlist)
WS2 — 6 terminal response sites + scattered redaction; target for one _resolve_final_response chokepoint
WS3 — single result-normalization seam for in-bound scrub emplacement (a); NOT hooked
WS3 — shipped flat-marker scrubber to evolve into policy OutputScrubber
WS3 — channel egress hop (emplacement b); JSON-safety only, no redaction
WS1/Q4 — prompt-layer forbidden IO list to promote into deterministic enforcement
Foundation — reuse target; must relocate down into core (parrot_tools -> core dependency direction)
Foundation — established home for relocated engine + new PythonCodeSanitizer/OutputScrubber
WS1 — in-process REPL shared-state coupling that keeps subprocess deferred

constraints.constraint: Dependency direction is parrot_tools -> core; core cannot import shell_tool. Any shared engine MUST relocate into core.
constraints.implication: Reusing CommandSanitizer/SecurityPolicy means moving them down into parrot.security and re-exporting to parrot_tools.

constraints.constraint: Secrets stay in os.environ (Q1 closed): python-dotenv + K8S inject. Cannot swap os.environ for the REPL.
constraints.implication: The env-access gate (WS1) and the in-bound scrubber (WS3) are LOAD-BEARING, not redundant.

constraints.constraint: data_analysis REPL shares in-process namespace (globals injection + DataFrame getter).
constraints.implication: Subprocess isolation breaks both paths without a state channel; stay in-process for FEAT-252.

constraints.constraint: AbstractTool.execute() is the single seam where every tool's raw output becomes a ToolResult.
constraints.implication: Hooking the scrubber once here covers all tools (including python_repl) — the brainstorm's emplacement (a).

constraints.constraint: A tactical fix is already committed on dev (0f76129b1) with tests.
constraints.implication: FEAT-252 is a consolidation/hardening of existing code, not a greenfield build; must not regress the shipped denylist or redaction.

whats_new: PythonExecutionPolicy + PythonCodeSanitizer (allowlist-first AST gate, general vs data_analysis profiles) — replaces/augments the shipped denylist [F002].
_resolve_final_response chokepoint in the Gemini client: one deterministic exit gate (provenance/echo + scrub) all 6 terminal sites funnel through [F003].
OutputScrubber (policy-driven, reason-tagged, audited, idempotent) hooked once at AbstractTool.execute() [F004], evolved from redaction.py [F005].
Relocation of shell_tool CommandSanitizer/SecurityPolicy/SecurityLevel into core parrot.security as the shared engine [F001, F009].
what_changes: pythonrepl.py: denylist -> allowlist-first gate (or hybrid); promote bots/data.py forbidden IO into deterministic denial [F002, F007].
google/client.py: remove the ~14 scattered redact_* calls; route all terminal text through _resolve_final_response; gate default_api/tool_code; decide empty-after-tools policy [F003].
security/redaction.py: evolve flat [REDACTED] into ***REDACTED:<reason>*** with audit log + allowlist-awareness, or keep lean (decision) [F005].
bots/base.py egress reuses the scrubber for TELEGRAM/MSTEAMS [F006].
system prompt: explicit closed tool manifest (no default_api) [F003].
whats_untouched: Moving secrets out of os.environ (Q1, deferred).
Subprocess/seccomp isolation of the REPL (deferred; in-process coupling [F008]).
Credential rotation / log purge (operational, out of code scope).
patterns_to_follow: shell_tool SecurityPolicy.restrictive()/SecurityLevel + ValidationResult verdict shape for the Python gate [F001].
Single-seam interception at AbstractTool.execute() rather than per-call-site [F004].
integration_risks: Allowlist too tight breaks legitimate pandas/numpy analysis -> needs calibration over real agent usage [F002].
Refactoring 6 from_gemini exits into one chokepoint risks behavioral drift in ask_stream latency / streaming [F003].
Relocating shell_tool primitives can break parrot_tools imports if re-export is incomplete [F001].

confidence_map.claim: A tactical fix is already committed on dev (0f76129b1) touching all the brainstorm's files
python_repl shipped as a DENYLIST (_check_ast_security + BLOCKED_*), not the allowlist-first PythonCodeSanitizer
confidence_map.reasoning: block lists + walk logic read directly

confidence_map.claim: No _resolve_final_response chokepoint exists; redaction scattered across ~14 sites and 6 from_gemini terminals
confidence_map.reasoning: grep absent for the method; 6 terminal sites + redact sites enumerated

confidence_map.claim: AbstractTool.execute() is the single result seam for in-bound scrub emplacement (a), and is not hooked
confidence_map.reasoning: execute() normalization branches + return read; no scrub call present

confidence_map.claim: security/redaction.py is a flat-marker standalone module, not the policy OutputScrubber, and not built on shell_tool engine
Shared engine must relocate into core because parrot_tools -> core dependency direction forbids upward import
confidence_map.reasoning: dependency direction is the documented convention; not re-verified against an import graph

confidence_map.claim: bots/data.py forbidden IO patterns are prompt-prose only, promotable to deterministic denial
confidence_map.reasoning: prose located in a system-prompt string

confidence_map.claim: In-process isolation is correct near-term because data_analysis shares REPL namespace state
confidence_map.reasoning: globals injection + locals getter read directly

unknowns.question: WS1 posture: keep the shipped denylist, or invest in the allowlist-first PythonCodeSanitizer + profiles the brainstorm argues for (anti-arbitrariness vs effort/calibration cost)?
unknowns.user_answer: Allowlist-first gate — build PythonCodeSanitizer/PythonExecutionPolicy (general vs data_analysis profiles), deny-by-default.

unknowns.question: WS2 scope: adopt the single _resolve_final_response chokepoint (consolidate 6 from_gemini exits, remove the 14 scattered redact calls, gate default_api) and which empty-after-tools policy — revive forced synthesis (latency) or return a typed 'no answer'?
unknowns.user_answer: Single _resolve_final_response chokepoint: consolidate the 6 from_gemini exits, remove the 14 scattered redact calls, gate default_api, and return a typed 'no answer produced' on empty-after-tools (no forced-synthesis latency).

unknowns.question: WS3 depth: evolve redaction.py into the policy-driven OutputScrubber (reason tags + audit + allowlist-awareness) hooked at AbstractTool.execute(), or keep the lean module and only add the single seam?
unknowns.user_answer: Evolve redaction.py into a policy-driven OutputScrubber (reason-tagged + audit-logged + allowlist-aware) hooked ONCE at AbstractTool.execute().

unknowns.question: Foundation: relocate shell_tool CommandSanitizer/SecurityPolicy into core and re-export, or accept code duplication in core (brainstorm allows duplication if extraction is awkward)?
unknowns.user_answer: Relocate CommandSanitizer/SecurityPolicy/SecurityLevel down into parrot.security and re-export to parrot_tools (single source of truth).

recommended_next_command.rationale: Localization is high-confidence and the seams are verified (C1-C5, C7-C8), but FEAT-252 carries four genuine design decisions (U1-U4) about how far to harden each workstream beyond the shipped tactical fix. A spec is the right vehicle: it captures those decisions as acceptance criteria and pins the contract per workstream. The codebase contract is already verified, so /sdd-brainstorm would be redundant.
